sglang.title: Add `set_var` to interpreter.py
sglang.author: 1024th
sglang.bodyExcerpt: This is useful when the user wants to define and return some extra infomation inside a sglang.function decorated function.

vllm.title: Update requirements-dev.txt to include package for benchmarking scripts.
vllm.author: wangchen615
vllm.bodyExcerpt: To add aiohttp==3.9.3, which is required for benchmarking scripts.

vllm.title: Separate attention backends
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR refactors the attention layer. Specifically, it separates the code paths for Ampere or more recent NVIDIA GPUs (which can directly use FlashAttention) and other GPUs, so that the code for the former becomes much simpler. This PR will also bring some performance improvements for ALiBi models,

vllm.title: Measure model memory usage
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt: There is already a measure for kv cache blocks memory usage, indirectly through how many blocks were allocated, but no direct measure of how much memory the model weights are using. This PR tries to add that by wrapping the model loading with torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated() calls. I'm not sure how

vllm.title: Possible fix for conflict between Automated Prefix Caching (#2762) and multi-LoRA support (#1804)
vllm.author: jacobthebanana
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Ensures the LoRA ID is a part of the hash used for prefix blocks.

vllm.title: Fix auto prefix bug
vllm.author: ElizaWszola
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Resolves #3193
Fixes a bug that occurs when the entire prompt has already been computed. If the entire prompt is marked as computed, the model runner will attempt to create zero-sized tensors for non-computed prompt tokens. This throws an exception in torch.arrange.
This fix is somewhat of a band-ai

vllm.title: Connect engine healthcheck to openai server
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt: @Yard1 added an engine health check in #3015, it would be useful to use this in the http /health endpoint, e.g. for use by Kubernetes health probes.

vllm.title: Feature add lora support for Qwen2
vllm.author: whyiug

vllm.title: [Minor Fix] Fix comments in benchmark_serving
vllm.author: gty111
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
In benchmark_serving.py, --model is an required argument, while --tokenizer isn't a necessary argument.
When executing the following command
python benchmarks/benchmark_serving.py \
        --backend <backend> \
        --tokenizer <your_model> --dataset <target_dataset> \
        --request-rate <re
